summary: Well-structured product-ideal blueprint that clearly defines a dashboard plugin model with clean topology (shell, gateway, plugin registry, feature plugin) and explicit causal chains for UI loading and backend proxying. Minor issues: a comment line violates the language root's rejection of comments, and the prescribes block partially restates constraints already in enforces, reducing net density. Local recoverability is high—a reader can fully reconstruct the plugin architecture from this file alone. Live-use evidence is uncertain without codebase confirmation.
strengths: Strong local recoverability: the file is fully self-contained as a product-ideal specification with clear defines, enforces, and when/then causality
Clean entity decomposition across Dashboard, Feature, and Runtime namespaces avoids naming collisions and makes topology scannable
Causal chains for shell loading and gateway proxying express real architectural decisions (lazy UI default, explicit backend startup policy, session auth as gateway concern)
The enforces block encodes meaningful ownership boundaries: features own their plugin definitions, shell and gateway must not duplicate feature knowledge
Defines block gives concrete, low-slack definitions for key terms like BackendStartupPolicy, LazyUiLoad, and GatewaySessionAuth
weaknesses: Line 3 contains a comment ('// Dashboard Plugins') which the language root explicitly rejects; the role attribute on DashboardPlugins already carries this information
The prescribes block partially restates enforces constraints (e.g., feature-owned status, session auth as gateway concern), reducing net semantic density
GatewaySessionAuth is listed inside featurePlugin.contains but is defined as a gateway-level concern—this placement slightly blurs the ownership topology the file otherwise maintains clearly
No live-use evidence is recoverable from the file itself; cannot confirm whether this blueprint is actively consumed by implementation code or other blueprints
